question_text: A stretched rubber band stores 50 J of elastic potential energy. When released, it flies away with 40 J of kinetic energy. How much energy has been transferred to the thermal energy store due to friction and air resistance?
explanation: When the rubber band is released, the elastic potential energy is converted into kinetic energy and thermal energy. Using conservation of energy: total energy in = total energy out. The 50 J of elastic potential energy equals 40 J of kinetic energy plus the energy lost to heat through friction and air resistance. Therefore, 50 J - 40 J = 10 J transferred to the thermal energy store.
